title: pahudia puberula
intro: Pahudia puberula is a plant species, also known as the puberula or soft-leaved pahudia, that is native to the tropical forests of Sri Lanka. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues. The leaves and roots of the pahudia puberula plant have been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, among other ailments, due to their health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In traditional medicine, the plant can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
